# Transcrição: 01-Understanding Business Manager 

- **Fonte:** 01-Understanding Business Manager 
- **Duração:** 02:03
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:03]

Business manager is going to be a very, very important tool as you grow and as you scale your Facebook advertising agency and even if you're not running a Facebook ads agency, it's going to be critical for your own business as you continue to grow as well. So it's great to give people access so that you don't have to hand out passwords, people don't have to use their personal profiles to do so and basically it just brokers relationships between Facebook assets and your team. So you're able to set up and allocate where you want people to have control and where you don't want people to have control and you can assign various assets based on that. So essentially it's just one hub to manage all pages, all ad accounts and people in one spot. So it's essentially just a hub and something that is used to broker the relationships that you have within either your agency or your business or within your business and the contractors or the agencies that you used hire to manage your ads for you. So it has its own URL and that URL is business.facebook.com and you'll be redirected to a page that is essentially asking you to set up a business manager account if you don't have that yet. So if you look here you have people, you have partners, you have pages, you have ad accounts and then there's a number of other things here, data sources, brand safety, a whole bunch of this but really I don't want you to focus too much on that right now. Really what you should be focusing on is people, partners, pages and ad accounts and we're going to dive into exactly what each of those will help you with and each of those will do but ultimately the business manager will just help you broker all those relationships within either your agency or within your business. So you're going to be able to request access to your clients pages, to your clients ad accounts and essentially assign those partners or those people on your team to those ad accounts as well. So we're going to dive into that and more in the next video.
